economic_notes: First Nation holds legal ownership of SAY Lands building jointly with Aitchelitz and Yakweakwioose First Nations. Investments in forestry, property management, and environmental monitoring through government business partnerships and enterprises. Member of Sto:lo Nation.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). ISC funding spiked dramatically from $5.05M to $10.26M year-over-year. Large amounts due from related First Nations (Aitchelitz $1.86M, Yakweakwioose $1.86M) related to SAY Lands building project where Skowkale holds legal title but beneficial ownership is shared. Deferred revenue increased significantly from $1.2M to $3.8M including $2.56M deferred ISC funding. Eight entities accounted for on modified equity basis as GBEs. Supplies line ($148,867) appears in expenses by object but not in the program expense breakdown. No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers estimated as ISC + FNHSA + MCFD + FNESC + Sto:lo Service Agency + School District #33 + Gaming (provincial) = $10,262,467 + $327,185 + $90,000 + $171,769 + $46,723 + $10,500 + $600,255 (other grants portion uncertain); conservative estimate used summing clearly identified government sources.
